title: Respond at personal-finance scale
description: Bound report generation, retained-history reads, policy validation, and recovery on the supported release host without changing correctness under load.
target: On the supported release host with 100000 active LEDGER transactions, 1000 category positions, 1000 retained Report Snapshots, and every active v1 policy, Generate and Retain each complete within 5 seconds at p95 and 256 MiB peak resident memory; Restate completes within 6 seconds at p95; Get, Status, Delete, and a 1000-item metadata List each complete within 1 second at p95; one-over-limit requests fail before financial output or retained-state change.
load_condition: Cold and warm one-shot invocations over sparse, maximum-page, maximum-category, maximum-history, correction, refund-heavy, and one-over-limit datasets on the documented supported release host.
measurement: Repeatable release-mode performance fixtures record end-to-end wall time, peak resident memory, producer time, calculation time, persistence time, and output size with p95 summaries and correctness assertions.
rationale: On-demand local analytics must remain convenient without introducing a daemon, replica, or correctness shortcut.
